content.intro: Welcome to PropertyGoose! This guide will walk you through setting up your account and getting familiar with the platform. PropertyGoose helps property managers and letting agents manage tenant references, tenancies, properties, and landlords all in one place.

steps.title: Register for an account
steps.content: Go to the registration page and enter your email address and create a password. Your password must be at least 6 characters long.

steps.title: Verify your email
steps.content: Check your inbox for a verification email from PropertyGoose. Click the link to verify your account.

steps.title: Complete onboarding
steps.content: After logging in for the first time, you'll be guided through the onboarding process. This includes setting up your company details, adding bank information, uploading your logo, and configuring your payment method.

sections.title: Understanding the Navigation
sections.content: The main navigation is located in the sidebar on the left. You'll find: Dashboard (your home screen with quick stats), Offers (send and manage tenant offer forms), References (create and track tenant references), Tenancies (manage active and draft tenancies), Properties (your property portfolio), Landlords (landlord records and AML), Standalone Agreements (generate AST agreements), Settings (account and company settings), and Help Centre (you're here!).

sections.title: Quick Create Menu
sections.content: Look for the '+' button in the header to quickly create a new Reference, Agreement, Property, or Landlord from anywhere in the app.

sections.title: User Roles
sections.content: PropertyGoose has three user roles: Owner (full access to everything including billing), Admin (full access except billing management), and Member (access to core features but not company settings, team management, or billing). Your role determines which features and settings tabs you can access.

sections.title: Multi-Branch Support
sections.content: If you're invited to multiple branches (companies), you'll see a branch selector after logging in. You can switch between branches using the 'Switch Branch' option.
